about-file.title: About.info
about-file.body:
boblbench is a personal site disguised as a tiny old computer.

The idea is to browse thoughts and projects as drawers, disks, and files instead of a normal scrolling blog feed.

now-file.title: Now.readme
now-file.body:
Current mission: build a convincing Amiga Workbench-inspired shell using only HTML, CSS, and JavaScript.

Short term goal: get the icon language and drawer hierarchy feeling right before adding real content.

bootlog-file.title: Bootlog.md
bootlog-file.markdown:
# Bootlog

A first pass at a **Workbench-inspired** web desktop.

## Milestones

- Static prototype established
- Visual language shifted toward Amiga Workbench 1.0
- Drawers and files now define the site structure
- Markdown viewer added as the first real application

## Why markdown?

Because it gives the site a nice split between:

1. old-school window chrome
2. modern readable writing

> The desktop is the shell. Markdown is the content.

### Tiny code sample

```js
const idea = 'browser-native desktop';
console.log(idea);
```

Visit <https://github.com/boblbobl/boblbench> for the source.

ideas-file.title: Ideas.txt
ideas-file.body:
Possible future additions:

- a startup chime and faux boot sequence

- icons that can be selected before opening

- a disk with archived years of writing

desktop-file.title: DesktopProject.doc
desktop-file.body: The desktop itself is the project: a nostalgic interface that still works as a readable public website.

links-file.title: Links.info
links-file.body: This can later become a curated set of internet shortcuts, references, and favorite corners of the web.

writing-file.title: Writing.md
writing-file.markdown:
# Writing

Writing probably belongs in **year/month drawers** so older posts feel archived instead of endlessly streamed.

## Structure idea

- `Writing/2026/March/`
- `Writing/2025/`
- `Drafts/`

### Notes

This first markdown app should eventually support:

- fenced code blocks
- links
- lists
- blockquotes
- inline code
